questionText: A ball is thrown so that, $t$ seconds after release, its position in metres is $x = 8t$ and $y = 20t - 5t^{2}$. Find the two times at which the ball is at a height of $15$ m, and the direction of motion (as an angle to the horizontal) at each of these times.

steps.explanation: The ball is at $15$ m whenever its vertical position $y$ equals $15$, so we solve this equation for $t$.
Bringing everything to one side and dividing by $5$ gives a tidy quadratic that is easy to factorise.
Two solutions are expected: the ball passes through $15$ m once on the way up and once on the way down.

steps.description: Differentiate to get the velocity components
steps.explanation: The direction of motion is the direction of the tangent, which we get from the two velocity components.

steps.description: Write the gradient of the path
steps.explanation: This ratio is the slope of the trajectory; its arctangent gives the angle the motion makes with the horizontal.
A positive gradient means the ball is still rising as it passes $15$ m on the way up.
Taking the inverse tangent of the slope gives the angle above the horizontal at which the ball is travelling.
A negative gradient means the ball is now descending as it passes $15$ m on the way down.
The size of the angle is the same as before, but it is now measured below the horizontal because the ball is falling.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The ball is at $15$ m at $t = 1$ s and $t = 3$ s. At $t = 1$ s it is rising at about $51.3^{\circ}$ above the horizontal; at $t = 3$ s it is falling at about $51.3^{\circ}$ below the horizontal.
